TextENGNew: The road back to the shrine.
I pedaled my bicycle alongside Keiichi.
The dim twilight that surrounded us when we parted ways with Rena had given way entirely to the deep of night, its presence settling like a blanket over everything in sight.
In the dead silence, the whir of our dynamo headlights illuminating the darkened road rang out with an almost obnoxious loudness.
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content from the original, indicating a misaligned or completely different passage.

TextENGNew: ``...I hope Rena's spirits recover soon.``

``She'll be fine.@k@vS14/01/210100967. I'm sure Mion will work something out.@k@vS14/01/210100968. When you're alone, your thoughts tend to spiral, but Rena should find it easier to open up to Mion about things.``
``...Mii. As expected of her fiance.``

``I keep telling you, that's not how it is.@k@vS14/01/210100970. ...Look, if it were you or me talking to Rena, she'd still be worried about keeping up appearances, right?@k@vS14/01/210100971. But Mion's older than us, and when it comes down to it, I think she'll really listen with an open heart.``
``Well, the financial damage wasn't all that bad, so the rest is up to Rena.``
Keiichi smiled as he said that, as if to sweep away all my anxieties. I'd thought he was being overly optimistic before, but... no. This was simply the faith Keiichi placed in his friends.
He gave everything he had to live up to the trust others placed in him. At the same time, he never forgot to place his own trust in them. And he never, ever underestimated them.
...Perhaps that sincerity was what made others feel his genuineness and want to repay his trust in kind.
I stole a glance at Keiichi's profile right beside me.
Ever since I arrived in this world, Keiichi's words and actions have caught me off guard time and again.

When my ability to foresee the future had made me overly defensive, he was the one who encouraged me. He shattered the pessimistic future I had resigned myself to with nothing but his convictions and his drive to act.
It wasn't the Keiichi of this world who declared that 'fate is as flimsy as a goldfish-scooping net,' but... he is unmistakably the same person he was then, deep down.
...And yet.
That was precisely why... I felt so uneasy.
It was a dark feeling—proof that I still hadn't placed my full trust in Keiichi and the others. But once that sense of dissonance had taken root, it wasn't something I could simply peel away.
``What's the matter? You were spacing out.@k@vS14/01/210100976. ...Are you okay?``
``I'm perfectly fine. Nipah~♪``
I answered with a smile plastered across my face.
...Come to think of it, I'd been putting on forced smiles around Keiichi a lot more often lately. No—not just around him. Around all of my friends.
``...Really?@k@vS14/01/210100978. Well, if you say so.``
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content from the original, indicating a misaligned or completely different passage.

TextENGNew: And as always, a wave of self-loathing came crashing over me right after. It felt as though I was forcing myself into a pose of false humility.
I already knew the real cause. ...It was this sense of alienation from everyone.
I knew the tragedy that lay ahead, but none of them did.
And though I was desperate to confide in them, I was certain none of them had any idea of the anguish and torment I carried inside.
...Those suffocating thoughts had given rise to a cynical version of myself—one that figured it was easier to just smile and play along.
I... loathed that version of myself beyond words.
We rode on in silence for a while, until the stone stairway of the Furude Shrine materialized from the darkness ahead. I stopped my bicycle beside it and kicked down the stand in my usual spot.
There was a lock on my bike, but I'd never once bothered to use it. Even if someone stole a bicycle in a village this small, it would be found and returned in no time.
...Of course, the culprit would receive a fitting comeuppance without fail.
``Well then, Rika-chan, see you tomorrow.``
Still straddling his bicycle, Keiichi flashed a cheerful smile and turned to leave. ...Even such a simple gesture struck me as strangely unsettling in my current state.
And so, I—
``——Please wait, Keiichi.``
``...Hm?@k@vS14/01/210100981. What is it, Rika-chan?``
``Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500628. ...There's just one thing I need to ask you.``
I'd already climbed four steps up the stairway and was about to raise my hand to say goodnight—but before I knew it, my true feelings had spilled out in words.
Keiichi froze, staring at me with a look of utter bewilderment.
It must have been a completely unexpected question—I knew that. But the feelings that had erupted from within me could no longer be contained.
``The Keiichi I know was someone with remarkable initiative and a sharp mind.``

``Because of that, he always thought things through on his own, made his own judgments, and accomplished a great many things.@k@vS14/05/210500633. ...But the flip side was that he'd act on his own, spiral out of control, and destroy himself just as often.``
``At the very least, he was never the kind of person who could trust his friends this deeply and act without a shred of hesitation.``
``...Rika-chan...``

``It's true that every time I rewound time, Keiichi would appear before me having grown just a little more.@k@vS14/05/210500636. When Rena went out of control, you saw your own past failures reflected in her, and in the end, you were able to stop her.``

``The same was true when Teppei took Satoko away.@k@vS14/05/210500638. You stopped Shion and the others from resorting to murder, rallied everyone to storm the government office, and even persuaded Oryou—ultimately rescuing Satoko.``
``So perhaps it's all right to believe in the kindness and strength of your heart.@k@vS14/05/210500640. ...But the you of this world—to me, you seem almost unnervingly different.``
I knew that already.
I knew full well that what I was saying was terribly rude, incomprehensible, and... utterly unreasonable.
Unlike me, Keiichi and the others didn't carry their memories across worlds.

So if I told him 'this is how you were in a previous world,' it would only confuse him. And there was no way the Keiichi of the present would be pleased to hear a third party like me tell him he seemed like a different person.
Even so.
Despite how consumed I was by my own anxiety, how could this person remain so strong, so calm? It was baffling, incomprehensible, and... more than anything else, I envied it.
I simply couldn't help but ask.
``Please tell me, Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500642. ——What is it that made the you of this world so strong?@k@vS14/05/210500643. How can you believe in your friends so unconditionally?``
``The Keiichi I knew wasn't nearly this strong.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1125. There were times he doubted, times he was afraid... and in the end...``
Keiichi stood still, listening intently to every word.
He didn't laugh, and he didn't get angry. He simply listened in solemn silence, his expression grave.
Watching him like that filled me with a strange mix of relief and irritation. ...Even though I recognized it as an inferiority complex, my heart refused to accept that understanding.
``————Rika-chan.``
After a moment.

Keiichi set his bicycle down, walked over to where I stood with my eyes cast downward, and slowly reached into his back pocket. He pulled something out and placed it in my hand. ...It was an omamori pouch.
Inside was something round and hard, like a bead.
``...Keiichi?``
When I looked up, Keiichi nodded with a gentle smile. Taking that as permission to look inside, I carefully opened the pouch and let its contents roll out onto my palm.
A dull gleam caught the moonlight, reflecting silver. It looked like a bullet from a gun... No—it was shaped exactly like a bullet.
``What is this...?``
``Exactly what it looks like—a pellet from a model gun.``
Keiichi said that with a lonely, self-deprecating smile. ...But he quickly lifted his gaze and turned to face me with a serious expression.
``There's something I'd like to talk to you about seriously.@k@vS14/01/210100988. ...Will you hear me out?``
``...Mii. I thought we were already having a serious conversation.``
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content from the original, indicating a misaligned or completely different passage.

TextENGNew: ``Ahahaha, sorry, poor choice of words.@k@vS14/01/210100990. Anyway, there's something I want you to hear.@k@vS14/01/210100991. ...Is that okay?``
I nodded in silence. Then Keiichi began to speak slowly, as though carefully weighing every word.
``...You remember how I said I went back to Tokyo recently for a relative's funeral?``
``The truth is, there was another reason I went.``
``Rika-chan, how much do you know about my past?``
``Specifically... about the trouble I caused back in Tokyo.``
``...I see. So you already know.``
``Then I'll skip the background... The truth is, I went to visit the home of the last person I shot.``
``Both my dad and my mom tried to talk me out of it, over and over.``
``The matter had already been settled with a monetary payment, and I'd already gone once with my parents to apologize.``
``Dredging it up now would only reopen old wounds. ...That's what they told me.``
``But I couldn't accept that.``
``In the end, I committed a crime and never truly atoned for it. Worse, I let my parents shoulder the burden of my redemption while I hid in their shadow like a coward.``
``...I just couldn't forgive myself for that.``
``All the people I hurt, their families, and my own parents too.``
``——The old me never once considered anyone else's feelings. I was a hopeless fool.``
``...I hated that version of myself. I was so pathetic, so ashamed...``
``...At the very least, I wanted to take back the responsibility my parents had shouldered for me—the sin that was originally mine to bear.``
``I was fully prepared to get punched as hard as they could hit me.``
``I was ready for the possibility that she wouldn't even agree to see me.``
``But as I rode the train, walked down the road, and drew closer and closer to her house...``
``...My courage drained away with every step, and my feet grew so heavy it was as if they were being sucked into the ground.``
``We already paid them. It should be fine.``
``I was caught by the police, and the settlement was agreed upon. It should be fine.``
``Besides, I already apologized to her once before...``
``...Every time I stopped at a traffic light or an intersection, I'd make another excuse to myself. I can't even count how many times I almost turned back.``
``Even so, I bore it, I endured it, I slapped my own face over and over—and finally... I made it to the front of her house.``
``I rang the doorbell.``
``...The sound rang out so loudly that I thought my heart would freeze solid.``

``When I heard a voice through the intercom... I had no idea what to say or how to say it. My throat was shaking, all the strength drained from my body, and tears just started streaming from my eyes.``
``Still, I somehow managed to wring out a voice and say my name—Keiichi Maebara.``
``...When I heard the person on the other end of the intercom gasp... I wanted to die right then and there.``
``I don't remember how long I waited after that.``
``It might have been an eternity, or it might have been an instant.``
``...But to me, it felt like it would go on forever—like waiting for a death sentence to be read.``
``...And in the end, it stretched on for so long, and I felt so utterly alone, that I began to wonder if it would ever end at all.``
``...Cold sweat poured from every inch of my body without stopping, and the world in front of me kept spinning and warping. It was pure agony—like being in hell.``
``And then...``
``I couldn't believe it.``
``I thought my own desperate wish was making me see things.``
``Because—``
``Standing on the other side of that door... was someone I could never forget... that very same girl.``
``I looked at her.``
``Her bandages were gone.``
``Her injuries had healed.``
``Just knowing that—it filled me with relief, and gratitude, and guilt all at once... I couldn't think straight anymore...``
``I broke down crying.``
``I crumpled to the ground right there and just wailed, squeezing the words out over and over—I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry...``
``Even after she helped me up... even after she invited me inside, that was the only thing I could say...``
``...I just kept repeating those words, desperately...``
``...And then, what happened?``
I'd been listening to Keiichi's story without interrupting, but I couldn't hold that question back.
From what I'd heard from a previous Keiichi, the girl had been seriously injured. If a simple apology could resolve everything, the world would have no need for police or courts.
And yet, Keiichi—
``I was braced for a beating from the girl's father... but he didn't hit me.``
``Instead, he scolded me harshly and lectured me sternly... Honestly, that was even harder to bear.``
His expression was strangely calm, a peaceful smile, as though some great weight had been lifted from him.

``Of course, crying and apologizing doesn't settle anything, and it certainly doesn't erase the sin I committed.@k@vS14/01/210101043. ...But at the very end, she told me.@k@vS14/01/210101044. 'I forgive you.'...``
...According to Keiichi, the girl's parents hadn't even wanted to let him see her.
She herself had originally planned to hurl every vile insult she could think of at him and turn him away at the door.
...But when she saw him apologizing so desperately, she found herself willing to listen... and in the end, a weight lifted from her heart, and she found it in herself to forgive him.
...I was only hearing this secondhand through Keiichi, but I could understand exactly how she must have felt.
It wasn't flowery rhetoric, and certainly not money or gifts—it was because he spoke from the heart that his genuine desire to make amends had reached her.
...Had Keiichi sought forgiveness armed with nothing more than his parents' settlement money, those people would surely have never forgiven him.
``And this is the pellet from the model gun that injured her eye.@k@vS14/01/210101046. Apparently she'd kept it all this time, but as I was leaving, she handed it to me and said—``
``Please promise me that you will never hurt anyone again.@k@vS14/00/josei10002. And will you hold onto this bullet, as a symbol of that oath?``
``So I swore it.@k@vS14/01/210101048. ...That I would never even think of doing something so foolish again.``

``My terrible behavior caused so many people pain.@k@vS14/01/210101050. So from now on, I'll protect the people around me and be someone who never hurts them.@k@vS14/01/210101051. That's the decision I made.``
``...Because I believe that's the only atonement I can offer.``
``Keiichi...``
I was speechless.
The Keiichi of this world hadn't become this way through some sudden mutation, as I'd first assumed.

Quite the opposite—he had confronted the sin he committed, reflected upon it, apologized for it, and even endured being berated for it... He had scaled the very wall that anyone would want to flee from, and that was why he stood here now.
I can't measure up to that... There's no way I could do what he did.
Listening to Keiichi's story, I felt the jealousy-like emotion I'd been harboring get overwhelmed and swept away, replaced by a profound sense of awe... and an indescribable feeling of defeat.
``Keiichi... that's truly admirable.@k@vS14/05/210500653. You chose to fight instead of running away, didn't you...?``
Even as the words left my lips, dark feelings stirred restlessly inside my heart.
You really are different from me. So strong, so kind... Nothing like a coward like me.
...And that's why...
``That's where you're wrong, Rika-chan.``
``By atoning for my sins, I learned the value of kindness and the importance of trust.@k@vS14/01/210101055. ...But the person who made that possible——was you, Rika-chan.``
``Mii? ...I'm... the reason?``
``Yeah.@k@vS14/01/210101057. ...Do you remember?``
And then, after Keiichi drew in a slow breath, the words he spoke—
—further betrayed my conviction that nothing could surprise me anymore.
``Rika-chan, you told me back then.@k@vS14/01/210101059. 'I forgive you, Keiichi.'``
``Pretty pathetic of me, wasn't it?@k@vS14/01/210101061. ...I wanted someone to forgive me so I could look away from my own wretchedness... I did something cowardly just to find comfort.``
``But Rika-chan, you knew all of that, and you still forgave me.@k@vS14/01/210101063. ...That's what finally gave me the courage to go apologize to the person I hurt.``
It can't be. That's impossible. A coincidence... no, that's not it...! Those words—I'm certain I said them to Keiichi in a previous world...?!
I want to believe it. I can't believe it. I want to believe it. I can't believe it. ...But I want to believe...!
Has a miracle like this ever happened before?
Keiichi—
The Keiichi from that time—
Could it be that he carried those memories across... and has them here, in this world...?!
I no longer cared who might be watching.
...I simply cried. I wanted to transform my sins into tears and wring every last drop from my eyes.
But the tears that fell were not my sins.
...They were only tears of remorse—grief over something that could never be undone.
And then... I felt a hand gently patting my head.
...When I opened my eyes, Rika-chan was stroking my head with her palm.
...I didn't need consolation.
There was no way Rika-chan could possibly comfort me over this.
But then, Rika-chan spoke.
...These weren't words of consolation.
``...I forgive you, Keiichi.``
``...Keiichi, you recognized your own sin.@k@vS14/05/170500200. That is... something truly remarkable... something most people could never do.``
``That's why no one else can understand how extraordinary that is, and so they cannot forgive you.@k@vS14/05/170500202. ...But I can.@k@vS14/05/170500203. I understand how incredible you truly are.``
``...Rika-chan...``
``...I know this for what it is—a miracle.``

``Because this should be impossible.@k@vS14/05/170500206. For you to become aware of a sin committed by another version of yourself—that is something that should absolutely never happen.@k@vS14/05/170500207. And yet, you did.``
``That is surely something so precious... that it would put to shame any other miracle ever to grace this earth.``
``It was because of what you said to me then... that I was able to save Rena.``
``And it's why I was able to atone for my sins in this world, find salvation... and keep my unwavering faith in everyone.@k@vS14/01/210101068. ...It's all thanks to you, Rika-chan.``
``...Thank you, Rika-chan.@k@vS14/01/210101070. And I'm sorry... for taking so long to remember.@k@vS14/01/210101071. I'd forgotten it all until I saw Rena like that...``
No words would come. My throat felt as though it had sealed shut, painfully tight, and I couldn't hold back the surge of emotion rising from within.
Could this be... what they call a miracle? Keiichi actually remembered... what I told him back then...?

``Would you let me repay you now... for what you did for me back then?@k@vS14/01/210101073. Before, I said I'd help if there was anything I could do, but that's not enough anymore.@k@vS14/01/210101074. I want to be your strength.``
``Rika-chan—you saved me when I despised myself. So won't you let me search alongside you for a way to make you happy?``
``...K-Keiichi... Keiichiii...!!``
Keiichi gently patted my head with a warm, kindhearted smile.

Looking into that peaceful face, I felt a burning shame and anger at myself for being so guarded, so unable to simply say 'help me'—and above all, a deep disgrace at having doubted even a fraction of Keiichi's sincerity.
``I'm sorry... I'm sorry, Keiichi...!@k@vS14/05/210500674. I said so many terrible things to you.@k@vS14/05/210500675. I thought so many terrible things about you.``
``Your kindness frightened me. I couldn't bring myself to believe in it. I looked at you with suspicion...@k@vS14/05/210500677. I'm sorry... I'm so sorry...!!``
``I should be apologizing too... I'm sorry.@k@vS14/01/210101077. In those previous worlds, I must have caused you all sorts of trouble, right?``
``I can't recall the details clearly... but just as you said, I'm sure I doubted everyone, cast myself as the tragic hero, and ended up bringing ruin down on myself.``
``...So this time, I want to do things right.@k@vS14/01/210101080. ...Rika-chan, will you let me help?``
``...Yes... Yes, I will...!@k@vS14/05/210500679. Having you with me gives me so much courage...!``
``I thought I had to fight this world all alone, and for so long—!@k@vS14/05/210500681. It was so painful, all this time...!!``
``You're not alone, Rika-chan.@k@vS14/01/210101082. Mion, Rena, Satoko, Shion—they're all your allies.``

``So when the time comes, let's tell them everything and bring them all into this fight together.@k@vS14/01/210101084. We can break through.@k@vS14/01/210101085. Because fate is as flimsy as a goldfish-scooping net!``
``...Ah, ahaha... Ahahahaha!!!``
He said it again...! He said that exact same line once more.
I'll believe...! Even without Hanyuu, even though I was anxious no matter how much things seemed to improve in this world—if he's here, we can manage somehow... No, I'm certain we will!
``Keiichi——``
``——Rika-chan.``
From my perch on the stone steps, I reached down and touched Keiichi's cheek. He let the hand that had been stroking my head slide gently down to my cheek, smiling all the while.
It was in place of a handshake. A kind of ritual—a mutual acknowledgment that we recognized each other as someone worthy of trust.

``I'll tell you everything I've kept hidden.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1125b. ...So will you save me?@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1125c. From this prison of hell—this endless cycle of reincarnation—will you save me, and all the friends I hold dear?``
``Yeah. Of course I will.``
Thank you. ...After saying that, I began to unspool the words I had locked away deep inside my heart.
``——Our enemy is Miyo Takano.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1127. Every so-called curse that has befallen Hinamizawa is a conspiracy—deliberately orchestrated by human hands.``
And so, slowly, I began to tell Keiichi everything.
About the horrifying incidents and the chain of misfortunes I had witnessed across all the worlds that came before——
